role: Deputy leader of Freedom Movement of Iran; Islamic thinker; reformist politician
bio: Abdolali Bazargan is an Iranian liberal politician, writer, and Islamic intellectual, and the son of [Mehdi Bazargan](/people/mehdi-bazargan), Iran's first post-revolution prime minister. He joined the National Front of Iran in 1961 before moving to his father's [Freedom Movement of Iran](/orgs/freedom-movement-of-iran) in 1977, where he currently serves as deputy leader. He co-authored the January 2010 [Green Movement](/events/green-movement-protests-2009) manifesto with [Abdolkarim Soroush](/people/abdolkarim-soroush), [Mohsen Kadivar](/people/mohsen-kadivar), [Akbar Ganji](/people/akbar-ganji), and [Ataollah Mohajerani](/people/ataollah-mohajerani), calling for the resignation of President Ahmadinejad and free elections under independent supervision.
editorial_notes: Born 14 August 1943. Son of Mehdi Bazargan. Deputy leader of Freedom Movement of Iran. Alive as of April 2026.
